title: Stryker 1688 Camera System - Camera Head Not Recognized
description: Identifies causes when the camera head is not detected by the console, preventing video output and delaying surgical procedures.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is actively undergoing a procedure with the affected camera. Move the patient to a backup camera system if needed.

steps.title: Verify Camera Head Connection
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect the camera head to the scope cable or console interface. Ensure the connection is fully seated; loose connections are a common cause. Expected outcome: camera is detected by console.

steps.title: Inspect Cables and Adapters
steps.instructions: Check for visible damage to the scope cable, camera head connector, and adapters. Swap with a known-good cable if available. Expected outcome: camera detection restored.

steps.title: Check Console Settings
steps.instructions: Ensure the console is set to the correct input/source for the 1688 camera head. Incorrect input selection can prevent recognition. Expected outcome: console recognizes camera head.

steps.title: Power Cycle Console and Camera
steps.instructions: Turn off the console and camera head, wait 30 seconds, and power back on. Expected outcome: system self-test completes and camera is recognized.

steps.title: Test with Alternate Camera Head
steps.instructions: If available, attach a different, known-good 1688 camera head. Expected outcome: if recognized, original camera head may be defective.

steps.title: Firmware and Compatibility Check
steps.instructions: Confirm console firmware supports the camera head model. Outdated firmware can cause recognition errors. Expected outcome: system operates normally if firmware is compatible.

CCR.Complaint: Camera head not detected by 1688 console during setup.
CCR.Cause: Camera head connection loose; initial cable inspection and swap confirmed issue.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected cable and replaced damaged cable; camera head recognized and tested successfully.

helpfulDetails: Connection ports inspected for debris or damage

title: Stryker 1688 Camera System - White Balance or Color Calibration Failure
description: Identifies causes when the camera image shows incorrect color, tint, or fails white balance, affecting visualization during procedures.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is actively undergoing a procedure with the affected camera. Move the patient to a backup camera system if needed.

steps.title: Verify Camera Head and Connections
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect the camera head. Check for bent or damaged pins on the connector. Expectation: Secure connection should restore communication for color calibration.

steps.title: Inspect Light Source and Fiber Optic Cable
steps.instructions: Ensure the light source is turned on and functioning. Swap fiber optic cable with a known working one. Expectation: Proper illumination is necessary for accurate white balance.

steps.title: Perform White Balance Procedure
steps.instructions: Follow the system’s on-screen procedure for white balance using a sterile white reference card or object. Expectation: The camera should adjust colors correctly; if not, an error may display.

steps.title: Check Camera Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm color mode and presets are correct. Reset settings to factory defaults if needed. Expectation: Misconfigured settings can cause color shifts.

steps.title: Test with Alternate Camera Head or Console
steps.instructions: Swap camera head with a known working head or connect the current head to another console. Expectation: Determines if issue is head-specific or console-related.

CCR.Complaint: OR staff reported the camera image appeared discolored and failed white balance.
CCR.Cause: Camera head connection and light source checked; settings confirmed; issue persists.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Fiber optic cable swapped and tested.
Light source verified to output correct intensity.
Console and camera head indicators normal.
White balance procedure attempted; error persists.

title: Stryker 1688 Camera System - Image artifact or quality loss
description: Identifies causes when video displays distortions, flickering, lines, or overall degraded image quality, affecting procedural visualization.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is actively undergoing a procedure. Move the patient to a backup camera to avoid compromising care.

steps.title: Inspect Camera Head and Lens
steps.instructions: Check for smudges, debris, moisture, or scratches on the lens. Clean with approved lens wipes. Artifacts often originate from external lens contamination.

steps.title: Verify Cable Connections
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect all camera and light cables. Poor connections can cause intermittent signal loss or visual artifacts.

steps.title: Test with Alternate Camera Head
steps.instructions: Swap the camera head with a known working unit. If artifact disappears, the original head may be faulty.

steps.title: Check Light Source and Fiber Optics
steps.instructions: Inspect the light cable for kinks, frays, or damage. Poor illumination can create uneven image quality. Swap with a working light cable if needed.

steps.title: Adjust Console Settings
steps.instructions: Verify white balance, gain, brightness, and sharpness settings are correct. Incorrect settings can produce unnatural color, noise, or ghosting artifacts.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Turn off the camera system, wait 30 seconds, then power back on. Temporary software glitches can cause image anomalies.

steps.title: Test on Different Monitor or Display
steps.instructions: Connect the system to another OR monitor to rule out display device issues. Persistent artifacts on multiple displays indicate source problem.

CCR.Complaint: OR staff reported flickering lines and distorted colors during procedures.
CCR.Cause: Lens contamination and a frayed light cable were found during inspection.
CCR.Resolution: Lens cleaned and light cable replaced; image quality restored and system returned to service.

title: Stryker 1688 Camera System - Light Source Failure
description: Identifies causes when the camera light source fails, dimming or eliminating illumination and affecting procedural visualization.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is actively undergoing a procedure with the affected camera. Move the patient to a backup camera/light source to prevent procedural delays or hazards.

steps.title: Verify Light Source Power
steps.instructions: Ensure the light source unit is powered on. Check that the power switch is engaged and indicator lights are illuminated. Failure here may indicate a simple power issue.

steps.title: Check Power Connection and Outlet
steps.instructions: Confirm the light source is plugged into a functional outlet. Test the outlet with another device to ensure power availability.

steps.title: Inspect Light Cable and Connector
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect the light cable from the camera and light source. Look for bent pins, debris, or visible damage that could interrupt illumination.

steps.title: Swap Light Cable if Available
steps.instructions: Replace the light cable with a known working cable to rule out cable failure. Observe if illumination is restored.

steps.title: Verify Console/Camera Settings
steps.instructions: Check the camera system settings to ensure light intensity is not set to minimum or muted. Reset to default if unsure.

steps.title: Test Light Source with Alternative Camera
steps.instructions: Connect the light source to a different camera head to determine if the problem is the light unit or the camera head.

steps.title: Inspect for Overheating or Unusual Behavior
steps.instructions: Look for excessive heat, smoke, or unusual sounds from the light source. If present, discontinue use immediately and escalate.

CCR.Complaint: Light source not illuminating during procedure.
CCR.Cause: Light cable connector worn, preventing proper power delivery.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced light cable; verified illumination restored.

title: Stryker 1688 Camera System - Coupler or Scope Connection Errors
description: Identifies causes when the camera coupler or endoscope connection fails, preventing proper video signal and affecting surgical visualization.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is actively undergoing a procedure with the affected camera. Move the patient to a backup camera or scope to maintain continuity of care.

steps.title: Inspect Physical Connections
steps.instructions: Check the camera coupler and endoscope connectors for visible debris, scratches, or bent pins. Clean connectors with approved wipes; ensure proper alignment before reconnecting. Secure connection may restore video signal.

steps.title: Confirm Proper Coupler Engagement
steps.instructions: Verify that the coupler is fully seated and clicks into place on the camera head. Re-seat coupler carefully without forcing it. Camera recognizes scope; image returns.

steps.title: Swap Cables or Couplers
steps.instructions: Use a known working coupler and endoscope to isolate the faulty component. Replace one component at a time. Identify if issue is with coupler, scope, or camera head.

steps.title: Check Camera Head Recognition
steps.instructions: Ensure the camera console detects the camera head after reconnection. Observe for “camera connected” indicator or image display. Recognition confirms external connection integrity.

steps.title: Verify Console Settings
steps.instructions: Ensure the console input mode matches the connected scope type. Adjust input selection or reset camera settings if needed. Correct input restores normal image.

CCR.Complaint: Camera not recognizing endoscope; video output absent.
CCR.Cause: Coupler alignment loose and connectors partially obstructed.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned connectors, reseated coupler; camera now recognizes scope.

helpfulDetails: Checked for debris or bent pins

title: Stryker 1488 Camera System - Camera Head Not Recognized
description: Identifies causes when the camera head is not detected by the console, preventing video output and delaying procedures.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the camera is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup system before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Camera Head Connection
steps.instructions: Disconnect and firmly reconnect the camera head to the camera control unit (CCU). Ensure it is fully seated and locked. A loose connection is the most common cause. Expected Outcome: Camera is recognized and video initializes.

steps.title: Inspect Connector Pins and Port
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the camera head connector and CCU port. Look for bent pins, debris, or fluid contamination. Clean if necessary using approved methods. Why it matters: Damaged or dirty pins prevent communication. If resolved: Camera is recognized after reconnection.

steps.title: Check for Cable Damage
steps.instructions: Examine the camera head cable for cuts, kinks, or stretched sections. Look for signs of fluid ingress. Gently reposition the cable while connected to see if recognition is intermittent. Expected Outcome: Stable recognition without movement-related failure.

steps.title: Confirm Proper Console Power-Up Sequence
steps.instructions: Power cycle the system: Turn off the CCU. Wait 10–15 seconds. Power back on with the camera head connected. Why it matters: The system detects peripherals during boot. If resolved: Camera is detected during startup.

steps.title: Try a Known Good Camera Head
steps.instructions: Swap with a known working camera head. If the new head is recognized → original camera head is faulty. If not recognized → issue may be CCU or port-related.

steps.title: Try a Different CCU Port (if available)
steps.instructions: If the system has multiple ports or inputs, connect to an alternate port. Expected Outcome: Determines if the issue is isolated to a specific port.

steps.title: Check for Error Messages or Indicator Lights
steps.instructions: Observe the CCU display. Note any error codes or lack of status indicators. Document behavior for escalation.

steps.title: Verify Compatibility and System Configuration
steps.instructions: Ensure the camera head is compatible with the 1488 system and no configuration changes were made. Why it matters: Mismatched components may not be recognized.

CCR.Complaint: Camera head not recognized by Stryker 1488 system; no image displayed.
CCR.Cause: Faulty camera head identified after swap with known good unit.
CCR.Resolution: Removed defective camera head from service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Accessories swapped (camera head reseated, alternate camera head tested)

title: Stryker 1488 Camera System - White balance or color calibration failure
description: Identifies causes of incorrect color, tint, or failed white balance affecting image accuracy during procedures.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the camera is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup system before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Camera Has Proper Light Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the light source is on, connected, and providing adequate brightness. Low or inconsistent light will prevent proper white balance. Expected: Bright, stable illumination

steps.title: Check Scope and Coupler Cleanliness
steps.instructions: Inspect the scope tip and coupler lens for debris, fluid, or fogging. Contamination can distort color calibration. Clean using approved lens wipes if needed. Expected: Clear, unobstructed optics

steps.title: Confirm Proper Scope and Coupler Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure the scope is fully seated and locked into the coupler. A loose connection can affect image signal and color processing. Expected: Secure mechanical connection with stable image

steps.title: Perform Manual White Balance
steps.instructions: Use a proper white reference (sterile white gauze or approved card). Fill the entire screen with the white target. Press the white balance button on the camera head. Expected: Image briefly adjusts and returns with natural color tones. If successful, stop here.

steps.title: Check White Balance Conditions
steps.instructions: Ensure no shadows or glare on the white surface, camera is in focus, no colored objects in frame. Improper conditions will cause failed calibration.

steps.title: Verify Camera Settings
steps.instructions: Check for unusual image settings (gain, brightness, profiles). Reset to default if needed. Incorrect settings can skew color processing. Expected: Neutral image settings

steps.title: Swap Camera Head (If Available)
steps.instructions: Connect a known-good camera head to the console. If issue resolves → original camera head likely faulty. If issue persists → continue troubleshooting

steps.title: Swap Coupler and Scope
steps.instructions: Try a different coupler and scope to isolate the issue. Optical components are common contributors to color distortion. Expected: Normal color returns with known-good accessories

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Check camera head cable, connectors, and lenses for damage. Damage can affect signal quality and calibration ability.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported camera image had a yellow tint and would not white balance.
CCR.Cause: Contamination noted on scope tip affecting color calibration.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned scope and performed successful white balance; image returned to normal.

title: Stryker 1488 Camera System - Image Artifact or Quality Loss
description: Identifies causes of visual distortions, artifacts, or image degradation that may interfere with accurate surgical visualization.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the camera is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup system before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect Camera Head and Connections
steps.instructions: Check that the camera head is fully seated in the coupler. Inspect the cable for kinks, pinches, or visible damage. Expected Outcome: Secure, undamaged connections often resolve minor image distortions.

steps.title: Check Light Source and Intensity
steps.instructions: Verify the light cable is properly connected to the light source. Ensure the light intensity is set correctly and bulbs/LEDs are functioning. Why: Insufficient or uneven illumination can create artifacts.

steps.title: Examine Monitor and Display Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm the display monitor is set to the correct input and resolution. Reset monitor brightness/contrast to default. Expected Outcome: Improper display settings can mimic image quality loss.

steps.title: Perform a White Balance / Calibration Check
steps.instructions: Follow on-screen prompts for white balance with a clean, neutral background. Expected Outcome: Restores accurate color and reduces artifact appearance if miscalibration caused distortion.

steps.title: Swap Cables or Camera Heads (if available)
steps.instructions: Replace the camera head or cable with a known good unit. Expected Outcome: Determines if the artifact is caused by a specific component.

steps.title: Inspect for Lens Contamination or Damage
steps.instructions: Check for smudges, condensation, or scratches on the lens. Clean with approved lens wipes. Expected Outcome: Restoring a clean lens often resolves image blur or spots.

CCR.Complaint: Surgeon reports blurry image with streaks and spots during procedures.
CCR.Cause: Cable and camera head inspected; lens had minor smudges. Light source and monitor settings confirmed normal.
CCR.Resolution: Lens cleaned, cable reseated, and calibration performed; image artifact resolved.

title: Stryker 1488 Camera System - Light Source Failure
description: Identifies causes of insufficient, flickering, or no illumination that affects visualization during endoscopic procedures.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the camera system is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup system before proceeding.

steps.title: Check Power to Light Source
steps.instructions: Verify the light source module is powered on. Check power cords and confirm wall outlet is functional. Expected outcome: Light source powers on; indicator lights are lit.

steps.title: Inspect Fiberoptic Cable Connection
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect the fiberoptic cable between the light source and camera head. Ensure the connector is fully seated. Expected outcome: Light transmits through cable without flicker or dimming.

steps.title: Swap Fiberoptic Cable
steps.instructions: Replace the current cable with a known working cable to rule out internal cable damage. Expected outcome: If illumination returns, the original cable is defective.

steps.title: Check Light Source Lamp / LED Module
steps.instructions: Confirm lamp or LED module is correctly installed and seated. Look for burnt-out lamps or failed LEDs. Expected outcome: Lamp/LED emits full light output when activated.

steps.title: Review Light Source Settings
steps.instructions: Ensure intensity settings on the console or light source module are not set to minimum. Reset to default if needed. Expected outcome: Full adjustable light intensity is available.

steps.title: Test Camera Head Transmission
steps.instructions: Confirm camera head receives light by temporarily connecting to a known working light source. Expected outcome: Image is illuminated; no dark or uneven regions.

CCR.Complaint: OR staff reported no illumination from the 1488 camera system during a procedure.
CCR.Cause: Fiberoptic cable was partially disconnected; LED module correctly seated but intensity set to minimum.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected cable, adjusted light intensity, verified illumination restored.

helpfulDetails: Indicator lights on light source module

title: Stryker 1488 Camera System - Coupler or Scope Connection Errors
description: Identifies causes when the camera coupler or endoscope is not recognized, intermittently disconnects, or fails to establish a video signal during procedures.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the camera system is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup system before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Physical Connections
steps.instructions: Verify the coupler is fully seated in the camera head. Inspect the endoscope connector for debris, bent pins, or damage. Ensure the coupler cable is securely attached to the console or light source.

steps.title: Swap Known-Good Components
steps.instructions: Test with a different coupler or endoscope if available. Observe if the connection error persists with known-good components.

steps.title: Check Console and Port
steps.instructions: Inspect the camera console port for debris or damage. Reconnect the coupler and endoscope carefully, listening for a secure click or indicator light.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Turn off the camera system and light source. Wait 10–15 seconds and power back on. Confirm if the coupler and scope are now recognized.

CCR.Complaint: Camera head intermittently loses signal when attached to the endoscope coupler.
CCR.Cause: Debris in the coupler connector prevented full seating, causing intermittent disconnection.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned the coupler and reattached; verified full connection. System tested successfully.

helpfulDetails: Alarm or error messages displayed on console
Scope or coupler swapped during testing
Power behavior of the camera system during connection attempts
Environmental factors (sterile field, OR lighting, humidity)
Indicator lights on camera head or console

title: Stryker T/Pump Series Patient Warming System - Heater not reaching set temperature
description: Troubleshooting low or inconsistent warming output when the unit runs but does not reach or maintain temperature.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from active patient use and switch to an alternate warming method. Expected: No patient connected during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Power and Unit Operation
steps.instructions: Confirm the unit powers on, display is active, and no major fault alarms are present. Expected: Unit enters normal operating mode. If not → address power issue before proceeding.

steps.title: Check Set Temperature
steps.instructions: Verify the temperature setting is appropriate for clinical use. Why it matters: Incorrect or low setpoints can appear as heating failure. If adjusted and temperature rises → issue resolved.

steps.title: Inspect Blanket / Pad Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure the hose or pad is securely connected to the unit. Expected: Firm connection with no looseness. If loose or disconnected → reconnect and retest.

steps.title: Verify Correct Blanket Type
steps.instructions: Confirm the blanket/pad is compatible with the T/Pump system. Why it matters: Incorrect accessories can limit heat transfer. If incorrect → replace with proper blanket and retest.

steps.title: Check for Kinks or Obstructions in Hose
steps.instructions: Inspect tubing for kinks, compression, or blockage. Why it matters: Restricted airflow reduces heat delivery. If corrected and heat improves → issue resolved.

steps.title: Assess Airflow Output
steps.instructions: Feel for warm airflow at the hose outlet or blanket. Expected: Noticeable airflow and gradual warming. If weak or no airflow → possible fan or airflow restriction.

steps.title: Inspect Air Filter (if accessible externally)
steps.instructions: Check for clogged or dirty intake filters. Why it matters: Restricted intake reduces heater efficiency. Clean or replace filter if needed and retest.

steps.title: Check Ambient Conditions
steps.instructions: Ensure the unit is not in a very cold environment or exposed to drafts. Why it matters: Environmental factors can affect heating performance. If corrected → monitor for improvement.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Unit
steps.instructions: Turn off, wait 10–15 seconds, then restart. Why it matters: Clears minor control or sensor issues. If normal heating resumes → issue resolved.

CCR.Complaint: Unit not reaching set temperature during patient warming.
CCR.Cause: Obstructed airflow due to kinked hose limiting heat delivery.
CCR.Resolution: Straightened hose, verified airflow and proper heating, returned unit to service.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (in service vs out of service)

title: Stryker T/Pump Series Patient Warming System - Over-temperature safety shutoff activating
description: Troubleshooting over-temperature alarms and heater shutdowns on Stryker T/Pump systems caused by airflow, setup, or accessory issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from active patient use and transition to an alternate warming method. Expected: No patient connected during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Alarm Condition
steps.instructions: Confirm the unit is displaying an over-temperature alarm or shutting down heating automatically. Expected: Heater disabled with alarm indication.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Unit
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, wait 10–15 seconds, then power back on. Why it matters: Clears temporary control or sensor faults. If alarm clears and does not return → issue resolved.

steps.title: Check Set Temperature
steps.instructions: Verify the setpoint is within normal clinical range (typically ~37–41°C). Why it matters: Excessively high settings can trigger safety shutoff. If corrected and stable → issue resolved.

steps.title: Inspect Blanket / Pad Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure the hose and warming pad/blanket are securely connected and not kinked. Expected: Firm connection, no airflow restriction. If restricted → correct and retest.

steps.title: Verify Proper Blanket / Pad Type
steps.instructions: Confirm the correct Stryker-compatible blanket or pad is in use. Why it matters: Incorrect or incompatible accessories can cause improper heat transfer and overheating. If incorrect → replace with correct accessory.

steps.title: Check for Airflow Obstruction
steps.instructions: Inspect air intake and exhaust vents for blockage (linen, dust, positioning against wall). Why it matters: Restricted airflow causes internal overheating and safety shutdown. Clear any obstructions and retest.

steps.title: Evaluate Blanket Placement and Patient Load
steps.instructions: Ensure the blanket is not folded, compressed, or tightly tucked restricting airflow. Expected: Even airflow distribution across the blanket. If adjusted and alarm stops → issue resolved.

steps.title: Inspect for Moisture or Fluid Exposure
steps.instructions: Check for any fluid ingress around the hose connection or unit housing. Why it matters: Moisture can affect temperature sensing and safety circuits. If present → remove from service.

steps.title: Test with Alternate Blanket / Hose
steps.instructions: Swap with a known-good accessory set. Why it matters: Rules out accessory-related restriction or defect. If issue resolves → faulty accessory identified.

CCR.Complaint: Unit alarming with over-temperature shutdown during use in OR. Repeated over-temperature alarms despite correct setup.
CCR.Cause: Airflow obstruction from blocked intake vent due to linen placement. Faulty temperature regulation suspected after accessory and airflow checks passed.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared obstruction, verified normal airflow and operation, returned unit to service. Removed from service and sent to vendor for repair.

title: Stryker T/Pump Series Patient Warming System - Pad / Hose Not Detected or No Circulation
description: Troubleshooting no circulation or pad/hose detection issues causing lack of warming due to external flow restrictions or connection problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from active patient use and transition to an alternate warming method. Expected: No patient connected during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm symptoms such as no warming, low temperature, or unit running without effective heat transfer. Expected: Unit powered on but not circulating or heating effectively.

steps.title: Inspect Hose Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure both supply and return hoses are fully seated and locked into the unit and pad. Expected: Firm connection with audible/physical click. If loose or disconnected → reconnect and retest.

steps.title: Check for Kinked or Obstructed Tubing
steps.instructions: Trace the full length of the hose and pad tubing. Why it matters: Kinks or compression will prevent water circulation. If kink found → straighten tubing and retest.

steps.title: Inspect the Pad / Blanket
steps.instructions: Check for folds, compression under the patient, or visible damage. Expected: Pad lies flat with no restriction to internal channels. If damaged or overly compressed → replace pad and retest.

steps.title: Verify Water Level in Reservoir
steps.instructions: Check that the unit has adequate water per manufacturer fill line. Why it matters: Low water levels prevent proper circulation. If low → refill appropriately and retest.

steps.title: Check for Air in the System
steps.instructions: Listen for gurgling or inconsistent flow sounds. Why it matters: Air pockets can block circulation. Cycle the unit and gently reposition hoses to help purge air. If circulation improves → issue resolved.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Unit
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, wait 10–15 seconds, then power back on. Why it matters: Resets flow detection and control logic. If normal operation resumes → issue resolved.

steps.title: Test with a Known-Good Pad and Hose Set
steps.instructions: Swap with a known-good pad and hose assembly. Expected: Unit should circulate and heat normally. If resolved → original pad/hose set is defective.

CCR.Complaint: Unit running but not warming patient; staff reports no heat output.
CCR.Cause: Kinked hose preventing water circulation between unit and pad.
CCR.Resolution: Straightened tubing, verified flow restored, and confirmed normal heating operation.

helpfulDetails: Tubing checked for kinks or compression
Water level checked and corrected if needed
Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: Stryker T/Pump Series Patient Warming System - Temperature Sensor Calibration Drift
description: Displayed temperature does not match expected warming output or fluctuates, often due to setup, airflow, or accessory-related issues rather than internal failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from active patient use and transition to an alternate warming method. Expected: No patient connected during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Compare the displayed temperature with expected performance or an external thermometer if available. Why it matters: Confirms true sensor drift vs perception or workflow issue. If readings appear normal → issue resolved.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Unit
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, wait 10–15 seconds, then power back on. Why it matters: Clears temporary sensor or control faults. If readings stabilize → issue resolved.

steps.title: Check Set Temperature and Mode
steps.instructions: Verify the setpoint is within normal clinical range (~37–41°C) and correct operating mode is selected. Why it matters: Incorrect settings can mimic drift or instability. If corrected and stable → issue resolved.

steps.title: Inspect Hose and Blanket / Pad Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure hose connections are secure and the blanket/pad is properly attached. Expected: Tight connections with no leaks or disconnections. If loose → reconnect and retest.

steps.title: Verify Proper Airflow
steps.instructions: Check that the hose is not kinked, blocked, or compressed and that blanket airflow pathways are unobstructed. Why it matters: Restricted airflow can cause uneven heating and inaccurate sensed temperature. If corrected → retest for stability.

steps.title: Inspect Air Intake and Filter Area
steps.instructions: Check for dust buildup or blockage at the air intake/filter. Why it matters: Reduced airflow can affect heater performance and temperature sensing accuracy. Clean if needed and retest.

steps.title: Confirm Correct Blanket / Pad Type
steps.instructions: Verify that the accessory is compatible with the T/Pump system. Why it matters: Incorrect accessories can alter heat transfer and sensor feedback. If incorrect → replace and retest.

steps.title: Check for Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Ensure the unit is not exposed to drafts, vents, or unusually cold environments. Why it matters: External conditions can affect perceived or actual temperature stability. Adjust environment if needed.

steps.title: Observe for Consistency
steps.instructions: Run the unit and monitor whether temperature stabilizes over time. Expected: Gradual and stable approach to setpoint. If unstable or drifting continues → proceed to escalation.

CCR.Complaint: Unit reported inaccurate temperature readings and inconsistent warming.
CCR.Cause: Airflow restriction from kinked hose causing unstable temperature sensing.
CCR.Resolution: Corrected hose routing, verified stable temperature operation.

title: Stryker T/Pump Series Patient Warming System - Power Supply or Internal Heater / Circulation System Failure
description: Troubleshooting power loss or no heat output due to power, setup, or internal circulation system failures.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from active patient use and transition to an alternate warming method. Expected: No patient connected during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Plug the unit into a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Test the outlet with another device if needed. Expected: Reliable power available. If outlet is faulty → move to a working outlet and retest.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord and Plug
steps.instructions: Check for frayed cable, bent prongs, or loose connections. Ensure cord is fully seated in the unit. Why it matters: Damaged cords can mimic internal power failure. If damaged → replace cord and retest.

steps.title: Check Power Switch and Indicators
steps.instructions: Turn the unit on and observe for display, lights, or pump/fan activity. Expected: Normal startup indicators present. If no response → continue troubleshooting power path.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Unit
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, wait 10–15 seconds, then power back on. Why it matters: Resets internal control and safety circuits. If unit powers normally → issue resolved.

steps.title: Check for Alarm or Safety Lockout Conditions
steps.instructions: Look for over-temperature or system fault alarms. Why it matters: Safety circuits may disable heating or operation after a fault. If alarm clears after reset → monitor for recurrence.

steps.title: Inspect Hose and Pad / Blanket Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure hoses are securely connected with no kinks or leaks. Verify pad/blanket is properly attached. Why it matters: Restricted or improper circulation can prevent heating. If corrected and system resumes heating → issue resolved.

steps.title: Verify Fluid Level (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Check reservoir level per facility protocol (if unit design allows). Why it matters: Low fluid can prevent proper heat transfer and circulation. If low → correct level and retest.

steps.title: Verify Temperature Setting and Mode
steps.instructions: Confirm setpoint is appropriate (~37–41°C) and correct mode is selected. Why it matters: Incorrect settings can appear as heating failure. If corrected → verify warming output.

steps.title: Assess Circulation and Heat Output
steps.instructions: Run the unit and feel for warmth at the pad/blanket and check for signs of circulation (subtle vibration or flow). Expected: Gradual, consistent warming. If unit powers on but no heat or circulation is present → likely internal heater or pump issue.

steps.title: Check for Unusual Signs
steps.instructions: Look for abnormal noise (no pump sound or grinding), burning smell, or excessive heat at the unit housing. Why it matters: Indicates internal component failure. If present → stop use immediately.

CCR.Complaint: Unit would not power on / no heat output during patient use.
CCR.Cause: Internal heater or circulation system failure suspected after verifying outlet, cord, and setup.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent to Biomed/vendor for repair.

helpfulDetails: Any unusual noise, smell, or heat

title: Stryker Prime Stretcher - Drive motor not engaging
description: Troubleshooting a Stryker Prime stretcher that will not drive or move under power due to battery, brake, control, or external issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from active patient transport if drive is unreliable. Expected: Patient transferred or alternative transport arranged.

steps.title: Verify Drive Activation Attempt
steps.instructions: Confirm staff are attempting to engage the drive correctly (handle, button, or control interface). Expected: Proper activation method used. Why it matters: Misuse can appear as motor failure.

steps.title: Check Brake Status
steps.instructions: Ensure brakes are fully disengaged before attempting drive. Expected: Stretcher rolls freely when brakes are off. Why it matters: Engaged brakes will prevent motor movement. If corrected and drive works → issue resolved.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Charge Level
steps.instructions: Check battery indicator or status display. Expected: Battery has sufficient charge. If low or depleted → charge battery fully and retest. Why it matters: Drive motors require adequate battery power.

steps.title: Verify Battery Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect battery compartment to ensure battery is properly seated and connected. Expected: Secure connection with no looseness. If loose → reseat battery and retest.

steps.title: Check Charger / Charging History
steps.instructions: Confirm the stretcher has been placed on charge and charger is functioning. Test charger with another unit if available. Expected: Charger provides power and charges battery. If charger faulty → replace or repair charger.

steps.title: Inspect Control Handles and Drive Controls
steps.instructions: Check drive handle, buttons, or triggers for physical damage or sticking. Expected: Controls move freely and respond normally. Why it matters: Failed input devices prevent motor engagement. If damaged → remove from service.

steps.title: Observe for Indicators or Fault Codes
steps.instructions: Look for error messages, flashing lights, or audible alerts when attempting drive. Expected: Normal operation without fault indication. Why it matters: Faults may indicate safety lockouts or system errors.

steps.title: Check for Physical Obstruction or Resistance
steps.instructions: Inspect wheels, casters, and drive mechanism for debris or binding. Expected: Wheels rotate freely without resistance. If obstruction found → remove and retest.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Stretcher
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off (if applicable), wait 10–15 seconds, then power back on. Expected: System resets and drive function restores if issue was temporary.

CCR.Complaint: Stretcher drive motor not engaging during transport.
CCR.Cause: Battery found depleted and not holding charge.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced battery and verified proper drive operation.

title: Stryker Prime Stretcher - Battery not charging or shortened runtime
description: Battery not charging, rapid battery drain, or stretcher losing powered drive due to charging, connection, or external power issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from active patient transport before troubleshooting. Expected: Patient safely transferred or alternate stretcher in use.

steps.title: Verify Charging Attempt
steps.instructions: Confirm the stretcher is properly connected to its charging source or docking station. Expected: Charging initiated when connected. Why it matters: Misalignment or incomplete docking is a common cause of no charge.

steps.title: Check Power Source (Outlet or Dock)
steps.instructions: Plug another device into the outlet or verify dock power indicator (if applicable). Expected: Confirmed working power source. If no power → move stretcher to a known-good outlet or powered dock.

steps.title: Inspect Charging Cable / Dock Contacts
steps.instructions: Check for bent pins, debris, or worn contacts on charger or docking interface. Expected: Clean, intact contacts with proper alignment. If dirty → clean contacts and reconnect. If damaged → remove from service.

steps.title: Observe Charging Indicators
steps.instructions: Check battery indicator lights or display status when plugged in. Expected: Charging indicator active (light or display confirmation). If no indication → continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Stretcher
steps.instructions: Turn the stretcher off (if applicable), wait 10–15 seconds, then power back on while connected to charger. Expected: Charging status resets and begins. If charging starts → issue resolved.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Seating and Connection
steps.instructions: Access battery compartment (no disassembly beyond access panel) and ensure battery is fully seated and secured. Expected: Firm connection with no looseness. Why it matters: Loose batteries prevent charging and can cause intermittent runtime.

steps.title: Evaluate Battery Condition (Runtime Check)
steps.instructions: If charging occurs but runtime is short, operate the stretcher under normal load. Expected: Battery holds charge for typical operational duration. If runtime is significantly reduced → battery likely degraded.

steps.title: Swap with Known Good Battery (If Available)
steps.instructions: Install a known good battery and test charging and runtime. Expected: Normal charge and runtime restored. If resolved → original battery is defective.

CCR.Complaint: Stretcher battery not charging and losing power during transport.
CCR.Cause: Worn battery with reduced capacity confirmed by swap test.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced battery and verified normal charging and runtime.

helpfulDetails: Outlet or dock tested and verified
Battery swapped with known good unit
Final device status (returned to service or sent out)

title: Stryker Prime Stretcher - Trendelenburg or hi-lo function failure
description: Troubleshooting guide for a Stryker Prime stretcher not raising, lowering, or tilting due to power, control, or mechanical issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from active patient use before troubleshooting. Expected: Patient safely transferred to another device.

steps.title: Verify Function Attempt
steps.instructions: Confirm correct controls are used (pedal, side rail, or control panel). Incorrect use can mimic failure. If corrected and function works, issue resolved.

steps.title: Check Battery Charge Level
steps.instructions: Verify adequate battery charge. If low, charge stretcher and retest. Lift and tilt functions require sufficient power.

steps.title: Confirm Brake Status
steps.instructions: Ensure brakes are in the correct state for operation. Some systems inhibit movement if brakes are incorrect.

steps.title: Inspect Control Panels and Cables
steps.instructions: Check for damaged or unresponsive controls. Try alternate control if available to isolate issue.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions or Mechanical Binding
steps.instructions: Inspect lift columns and frame for debris or interference. Remove obstruction and retest.

steps.title: Listen for Actuator Response
steps.instructions: Listen for motor engagement. Motor noise without movement suggests mechanical issue; no noise suggests electrical/control issue.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Stretcher
steps.instructions: Turn off, wait 10–15 seconds, and power back on to reset system.

steps.title: Verify Charger Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure stretcher is connected to power. Some units limit movement when battery is critically low.

CCR.Complaint: Stretcher would not raise or go into Trendelenburg during patient transport.
CCR.Cause: Battery found fully depleted preventing actuator operation.
CCR.Resolution: Charged stretcher and verified full hi-lo and Trendelenburg functionality.

helpfulDetails: Any actuator noise present or absent

title: Stryker Prime Stretcher - Steering Failure
description: Stretcher not tracking straight, difficult to steer, or locked in one direction due to brake, wheel, or steering mechanism issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from active patient transport before troubleshooting. Expected: Patient safely transferred to another device.

steps.title: Verify Steering Mode Selection
steps.instructions: Confirm the stretcher is set to the correct steering mode (steer, neutral, or locked). Expected: Steering mode engaged properly. Why it matters: Incorrect mode is the most common cause of steering issues. If corrected and steering returns → issue resolved.

steps.title: Check Brake Status
steps.instructions: Ensure brakes are fully disengaged before attempting to steer. Expected: Stretcher rolls freely. Why it matters: Partial brake engagement can restrict wheel movement and mimic steering failure. If corrected and movement is normal → issue resolved.

steps.title: Inspect Steering Control Pedals / Levers
steps.instructions: Operate the steering pedal or control mechanism and observe response. Expected: Audible or physical engagement of steering mechanism. Why it matters: Misalignment or linkage issues can prevent proper steering activation. If control feels loose or unresponsive → note for escalation.

steps.title: Check Wheel Alignment and Orientation
steps.instructions: Visually inspect all casters for proper alignment and positioning. Expected: Wheels align when steering mode is engaged. Why it matters: Misaligned casters can cause pulling or inability to steer straight. If corrected manually and issue resolves → issue resolved.

steps.title: Inspect for Obstructions or Debris
steps.instructions: Check around wheels and caster assemblies for debris (hair, tape, etc.). Expected: Wheels rotate freely without obstruction. Why it matters: Debris can restrict caster movement and affect steering. If debris removed and steering improves → issue resolved.

steps.title: Test Movement in All Modes
steps.instructions: Cycle through steering, neutral, and locked modes while moving the stretcher. Expected: Clear difference between modes. Why it matters: Confirms whether the issue is mode-related or mechanical. If no change between modes → likely internal steering linkage issue.

steps.title: Check for Uneven Resistance or Drag
steps.instructions: Push the stretcher and note if it pulls to one side or resists movement. Expected: Smooth, even movement. Why it matters: Indicates potential caster bearing or steering linkage problems.

CCR.Complaint: Stretcher difficult to steer and pulling to one side during transport.
CCR.Cause: Debris found restricting front caster movement and steering response.
CCR.Resolution: Removed debris, verified proper steering function, and returned stretcher to service.

title: Stryker Prime Stretcher - Control Panel Errors
description: Unresponsive buttons, intermittent controls, or error messages caused by power issues, connection faults, or environmental factors.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from active patient use before troubleshooting. Expected: Patient safely transferred to another device.

steps.title: Verify Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to reproduce the problem (unresponsive buttons, incorrect display, intermittent function). Expected: Confirmed control panel issue. Why it matters: Ensures this is not a user operation error or isolated event.

steps.title: Check Battery Charge Level
steps.instructions: Observe battery indicator or display status. Expected: Adequate battery charge. If low → charge fully and retest. Why it matters: Low voltage can cause control panel malfunction or erratic behavior. If resolved → stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Power / Charging Connection
steps.instructions: If plugged in or docked, confirm proper connection and charging indication. Expected: Stable power input. Why it matters: Inconsistent power can cause control resets or errors. If corrected and panel stabilizes → issue resolved.

steps.title: Perform a Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Turn the stretcher off, wait 10–15 seconds, then power back on. Expected: Control panel reboots and functions normally. Why it matters: Clears temporary software or communication faults. If resolved → stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect Control Panel Surface and Buttons
steps.instructions: Check for stuck buttons, fluid ingress, physical damage, or worn overlays. Expected: Buttons move freely and respond when pressed. Why it matters: Mechanical issues are a common external cause of input failure. If damaged or unresponsive → remove from service.

steps.title: Check for Moisture or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect panel for cleaning solution residue or fluid exposure. Expected: Dry, clean surface. Why it matters: Moisture can cause shorting or false inputs. If present → allow to dry completely and retest.

steps.title: Inspect External Wiring / Harness (If Accessible)
steps.instructions: Check visible connections between control panel and stretcher frame (no disassembly). Expected: Secure, undamaged connections. Why it matters: Loose or pinched cables can interrupt control signals. If issue found → remove from service.

steps.title: Observe for Error Codes or Indicators
steps.instructions: Note any displayed error messages, blinking lights, or abnormal behavior. Expected: No active errors during normal operation. Why it matters: Error codes can help identify system-level faults for escalation.

CCR.Complaint: Control panel unresponsive and intermittently not adjusting stretcher functions.
CCR.Cause: Likely control panel failure after ruling out power, battery, and external connection issues.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent for further evaluation and repair.

helpfulDetails: Presence of moisture or residue checked
Final device status (operational or removed from service)

title: Stryker 6090 / 6500 Series Stretcher - Hi-Lo Actuator Not Moving or Slow
description: Hi-lo function not responding or moving slowly due to power, controls, or mechanical resistance issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from active patient use if height adjustment is unreliable. Transfer the patient to another safe surface.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Operate the hi-lo control and observe no movement at all, slow or uneven movement, or audible motor noise without movement.

steps.title: Check Power Source (If Powered Model)
steps.instructions: Plug stretcher into a known working outlet. Confirm any power indicators are illuminated. Verify power cord is intact and fully seated.

steps.title: Check Battery Condition (If Equipped)
steps.instructions: Attempt operation on battery power. Observe for weak or sluggish movement. Check for low battery indicators.

steps.title: Inspect Hand Control or Foot Pedals
steps.instructions: Test multiple control inputs (hand pendant and/or foot pedals). Check for stuck, damaged, or unresponsive buttons.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions or Load Issues
steps.instructions: Inspect under the stretcher for debris or objects restricting movement. Verify weight load is within normal limits. Remove excess weight if possible and retest.

steps.title: Inspect Actuator Movement and Sound
steps.instructions: Activate hi-lo while observing the actuator and listen for motor running with no movement, clicking or grinding, or no sound at all.

steps.title: Check Wiring and Connections (External Only)
steps.instructions: Inspect visible actuator cables and connectors. Ensure connections are secure and undamaged. Look for pinched or cut wiring.

steps.title: Perform Power Reset (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Disconnect from AC power, wait 30 seconds, reconnect and retest.

CCR.Complaint: Stretcher hi-lo function not moving when controls activated.
CCR.Cause: Actuator failed to respond due to internal motor degradation and weak battery condition.
CCR.Resolution: Removed stretcher from service and sent to biomed shop for actuator replacement and battery evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (in service or removed)

title: Stryker 6090 / 6500 Series Stretcher - IV Pole or Accessory Mount Failure
description: Loose, unstable, or non-functional IV pole or accessory mount caused by improper installation, worn components, or mounting hardware issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while the stretcher is in active patient use. Remove any IV bags or accessories from the pole before inspection.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Check what is failing: IV pole wobbling or loose, pole will not lock into place, mount bracket shifting or detaching, accessory (monitor, pump, etc.) not securing properly.

steps.title: Inspect IV Pole Seating and Installation
steps.instructions: Remove and reinsert the IV pole into the mount. Ensure it is fully seated and aligned correctly. Check for improper insertion, misalignment, and partial engagement.

steps.title: Check Locking Mechanism or Set Screw
steps.instructions: Inspect the locking knob, clamp, or set screw securing the IV pole. Tighten the knob or screw, ensure threads are not stripped, and confirm locking mechanism engages fully.

steps.title: Inspect Mounting Bracket and Hardware
steps.instructions: Check the mount where the IV pole or accessory attaches to the stretcher frame. Look for loose or missing screws/bolts, cracked or bent bracket, and worn mounting holes. Tighten any loose hardware as needed.

steps.title: Test with a Known Good IV Pole or Accessory
steps.instructions: Swap with a known working IV pole or accessory. If new pole works → original pole is defective. If issue remains → mount is the problem.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage or Wear
steps.instructions: Inspect both the pole and mount for bent metal, worn grooves, cracks or deformation, and excessive play even when tightened.

steps.title: Verify Weight Load and Usage
steps.instructions: Confirm accessories are within intended weight limits. Check for multiple pumps stacked and heavy equipment mounted improperly.

CCR.Complaint: IV pole loose and unable to securely hold infusion pumps during transport.
CCR.Cause: Worn locking mechanism and loose mounting bracket hardware identified.
CCR.Resolution: Tightened mounting hardware and replaced IV pole; confirmed secure attachment and normal function.

title: Stryker 6090 / 6500 Series Stretcher - Brake or Caster Locking Failure
description: Troubleshooting brake pedals not engaging, stretcher rolling when brakes are applied, or caster locking system failures.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from active patient use if brakes are not holding. Transfer the patient to a safe, stable surface.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Engage and disengage the brake pedal while observing caster behavior. Check if all wheels lock, if the stretcher is still able to roll, and whether the issue is intermittent or constant.

steps.title: Check Brake Pedal Linkage and Movement
steps.instructions: Operate the brake pedal and observe smooth travel, full engagement, and return to neutral position.

steps.title: Inspect for Obstructions or Debris in Casters
steps.instructions: Visually inspect casters for hair, tape, or debris. Remove any debris and retest.

steps.title: Check Individual Casters for Locking Function
steps.instructions: Engage brakes and test each caster individually to identify if any are not locking.

steps.title: Inspect Brake Rods and Linkages Under the Frame
steps.instructions: Visually inspect linkage rods for bending, disconnection, or loose hardware without disassembling the unit.

steps.title: Check for Uneven Floor Contact or Load Distribution
steps.instructions: Ensure the stretcher is on a flat surface and all wheels are in contact with the floor without uneven load.

steps.title: Test Brake Function Under Load
steps.instructions: Apply brakes and gently attempt to move the stretcher with weight applied to confirm proper braking function.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported stretcher brakes not holding and unit rolling when parked.
CCR.Cause: Debris buildup and one caster not locking properly due to worn brake mechanism.
CCR.Resolution: Removed debris, identified faulty caster, and removed stretcher from service for caster replacement.

helpfulDetails: Brake pedal travel normal or loose
Number of casters locking vs not locking
Final device status (in service or removed)

title: Stryker 6090 / 6500 Series Stretcher - Trendelenburg or Backrest Not Adjusting
description: Stretcher backrest or Trendelenburg function not moving, stuck, or slow due to mechanical, hydraulic, or control-related external issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from patient use if positioning cannot be adjusted safely. Transfer the patient to another stretcher if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to adjust both the backrest and Trendelenburg using normal controls (manual lever or pedal depending on configuration). Determine if nothing moves, only one function is affected, or movement is slow or jerky.

steps.title: Check for Mechanical Obstruction
steps.instructions: Inspect under the stretcher and around the backrest hinge and frame for linens caught in joints, debris in linkage points, or bent components or misalignment.

steps.title: Verify Control Lever/Pedal Operation
steps.instructions: Operate the Trendelenburg/backrest control levers or pedals and ensure full range of motion. Check for loose, broken, or disconnected linkage.

steps.title: Check Hydraulic System Response
steps.instructions: While activating the control, listen for hydraulic movement and observe if there is any slight movement or resistance.

steps.title: Inspect for Hydraulic Leaks
steps.instructions: Check around hydraulic cylinders, hoses, and pump assembly for oil residue, wetness, drips, or low reservoir levels.

steps.title: Check Load and Weight Distribution
steps.instructions: Ensure the stretcher is not overloaded or unevenly loaded. Remove excess weight and reattempt adjustment.

steps.title: Cycle the Function Multiple Times
steps.instructions: Attempt repeated full-range adjustments by raising and lowering the backrest fully and engaging Trendelenburg several times.

steps.title: Compare with a Known Working Unit (If Available)
steps.instructions: Operate another 6090/6500 stretcher for comparison to identify abnormal resistance or behavior.

CCR.Complaint: Stretcher backrest and Trendelenburg functions not adjusting per clinical staff report.
CCR.Cause: Hydraulic system not responding; no external obstruction found during inspection.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent for hydraulic system repair and evaluation.

title: Stryker 6090 / 6500 Series Stretcher - Battery or Power Backup Failure
description: Troubleshooting stretcher battery not charging, no power to electric functions, or backup power failure during transport.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from patient use if powered functions are needed. Transfer the patient to a safe, fully functional stretcher.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to use powered functions (hi-lo, backrest if powered). Check if the stretcher has no response at all, works only when plugged in, or shows intermittent power loss.

steps.title: Check AC Power Connection (If Equipped)
steps.instructions: Plug the stretcher into a known working outlet. Inspect the power cord for secure connection, damage or fraying, and loose inlet connection.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Charging Indicators
steps.instructions: Check for any battery or charging indicator lights. No lights may indicate no power or failed charger. Charging light present but no function may indicate battery failure.

steps.title: Check for Blown Fuses or Resettable Breakers (External Only)
steps.instructions: Inspect accessible fuse holders or reset buttons near the power entry point. Reset breaker if tripped and replace fuse only if visibly blown and approved.

steps.title: Perform a Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Disconnect from AC power, wait 30–60 seconds, then reconnect and attempt operation again.

steps.title: Evaluate Battery Condition
steps.instructions: If the stretcher only works when plugged in, loses power quickly after unplugging, or has no battery response, this indicates a failed or depleted battery.

steps.title: Check for Physical Signs of Battery Issues
steps.instructions: Without disassembly, inspect for unusual heat, swelling, deformation, or burning smell near the battery compartment.

CCR.Complaint: Stretcher will not power on or loses power when unplugged.
CCR.Cause: Failed internal battery not holding charge.
CCR.Resolution: Verified AC power, inspected connections, removed stretcher from service for battery replacement.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (in service / out of service)

title: Stryker SDC Ultra Endoscopic / OR Camera System - Image Capture or Recording Not Initiating
description: Failure to start image capture or recording due to storage, connection, control input, or system readiness issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If recording is required for documentation during a procedure, notify clinical staff and transition to a backup documentation method if available.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to initiate image capture or recording using the touchscreen, footswitch, or control interface and observe for no response, error messages, or inactive controls.

steps.title: Check Storage Media (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Verify that a USB drive or approved storage device is inserted, recognized, and not reporting storage full or no media errors.

steps.title: Verify Storage Capacity and Format
steps.instructions: Confirm available storage space and ensure the device is properly formatted (typically FAT32 or system-compatible format), replacing if necessary.

steps.title: Check Input Signal from Video Source
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is receiving a valid video signal by verifying monitor output and checking or reseating input cables.

steps.title: Check Control Method (Touchscreen / Footswitch)
steps.instructions: Test recording via touchscreen and footswitch, inspect connections, and swap accessories if needed.

steps.title: Confirm System is Not in Error or Standby State
steps.instructions: Check for system errors, ensure the system is fully booted, and clear any prompts preventing recording.

steps.title: Restart the System
steps.instructions: Power down the SDC Ultra, wait 10–15 seconds, power back on, and retest recording functionality.

CCR.Complaint: SDC Ultra not initiating recording during procedure.
CCR.Cause: USB storage device was full and not allowing new recordings.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced with cleared USB device and verified recording functionality.

helpfulDetails: Final device status verified operational or removed from service

title: Stryker SDC Ultra Endoscopic / OR Camera System - USB or External Storage Not Recognized
description: USB drives or external storage not detected or failing due to compatibility, formatting, or connection issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot storage devices during an active procedure. Ensure recording needs are transitioned to backup storage or system if required.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Insert the USB or external storage device and observe system behavior including no detection prompt, error message, intermittent recognition, or recording/export failure.

steps.title: Check USB Port and Physical Connection (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Remove and reinsert the USB device firmly, try a different USB port, and check for bent pins, debris, or loose ports.

steps.title: Test with a Known-Good USB Device
steps.instructions: Insert a known-working, hospital-approved USB drive to determine if the issue is with the original USB or the system.

steps.title: Verify USB Compatibility and Format
steps.instructions: Ensure the USB is formatted in a supported file system such as FAT32 or exFAT, avoid unsupported formats like NTFS, and confirm storage capacity is within supported limits.

steps.title: Check for Write Protection or Security Restrictions
steps.instructions: Disable any write protection and avoid using encrypted or password-protected drives.

steps.title: Review System Storage Settings and Menu Options
steps.instructions: Verify correct storage destination is selected, confirm external storage is enabled, and check for system messages regarding storage errors.

steps.title: Restart the System
steps.instructions: Perform a full system reboot with no USB device inserted, then reconnect after startup.

steps.title: Inspect for Environmental or Hardware Issues
steps.instructions: Check for loose or damaged USB ports, intermittent detection when moving the device, and whether multiple ports are failing.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported USB drive not recognized when attempting to record procedure on SDC Ultra.
CCR.Cause: Incompatible USB file system (NTFS) prevented system from recognizing external storage.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced with properly formatted FAT32 USB device; confirmed successful detection and recording.

title: Stryker SDC Ultra Endoscopic / OR Camera System - Touchscreen or Soft Key Failure
description: Troubleshooting unresponsive touchscreen or soft keys on the Stryker SDC Ultra due to external factors, software issues, or interface problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the system is being used during a procedure, notify staff and transition to an alternate control method or backup system. Expected: Procedure continues safely. Why it matters: Loss of control can delay documentation or interfere with workflow during procedures.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to use the touchscreen and soft keys. Observe no response to touch, intermittent or delayed response, or incorrect selections being triggered. Expected: Issue is reproducible. Why it matters: Confirms this is a true interface problem, not user error.

steps.title: Check for Screen Lock or Software State (Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Verify the system is not locked or in a restricted mode. Look for lock icons or prompts, attempt to exit menus, and restart the interface if accessible. Expected: Normal control restored if caused by software lock. Why it matters: Locked or frozen UI is a frequent cause of perceived failure.

steps.title: Perform a System Restart (Very Common Fix)
steps.instructions: Power cycle the SDC Ultra by shutting it down, disconnecting power for 10–15 seconds, and restarting. Expected: Touchscreen and soft keys respond normally. Why it matters: Clears temporary software or interface glitches.

steps.title: Inspect Touchscreen Surface
steps.instructions: Clean the screen and remove moisture, debris, or residue. Check for damaged or peeling protective film. Expected: Improved or restored touch response. Why it matters: Contamination can block or distort touch input.

steps.title: Check for External Input Devices or USB Interference
steps.instructions: Disconnect USB drives, keyboards, or accessories and retest. Expected: Input control returns to normal. Why it matters: External devices can interfere with input priority or responsiveness.

steps.title: Observe for System Performance Issues
steps.instructions: Check for lag, delayed menus, freezes, or near-full storage. Expected: Normal responsiveness. Why it matters: System overload can affect UI performance.

steps.title: Test Soft Keys Independently
steps.instructions: Press each soft key individually and check for feedback and response. Expected: Keys respond correctly. Why it matters: Differentiates touchscreen failure from key interface issues.

CCR.Complaint: Touchscreen and soft keys on SDC Ultra not responding during case setup.
CCR.Cause: Touchscreen unresponsive due to system software freeze.
CCR.Resolution: Power cycled system and verified normal touchscreen and soft key operation.

title: Stryker SDC Ultra Endoscopic / OR Camera System - Network or DICOM Export Failure
description: Troubleshooting failed image/video export, DICOM transmission errors, and network connectivity issues on the SDC Ultra.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If export is needed for documentation during a procedure, ensure the case is completed safely and data is stored locally if possible.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to export an image or video to PACS or network destination and observe failure type, delays, or error messages.

steps.title: Check Network Cable Connection (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Inspect Ethernet cable, ensure it is fully seated, check for damage, and verify link/activity lights.

steps.title: Verify Network Connectivity
steps.instructions: Check IP settings, confirm network status, test connection, and try a known-good network port.

steps.title: Confirm DICOM Configuration Settings
steps.instructions: Verify AE Title, IP address, port number, and ensure settings match PACS/server configuration.

steps.title: Test with Alternate Destination or Storage
steps.instructions: Attempt export to another configured destination or local storage to isolate the issue.

steps.title: Check External Network Availability
steps.instructions: Confirm with IT that network and PACS systems are operational and not experiencing downtime.

steps.title: Restart the System (If Safe to Do So)
steps.instructions: Power cycle the SDC Ultra to reset network interfaces and clear temporary faults.

steps.title: Inspect for Software Errors or Alerts
steps.instructions: Review system messages for network, storage, or DICOM-related errors.

CCR.Complaint: SDC Ultra unable to export procedure images to PACS; DICOM transfer failing.
CCR.Cause: Incorrect DICOM destination IP configuration identified during verification.
CCR.Resolution: Corrected DICOM settings and confirmed successful export to PACS.

title: Stryker SDC Ultra Endoscopic / OR Camera System - System Freeze or Boot Failure
description: Troubleshooting system freezing, unresponsive controls, or boot failure caused by power, peripherals, or startup issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the system freezes or fails during a procedure, immediately transition to a backup documentation system. Expected: Procedure continues without interruption. Why it matters: Loss of recording or display can impact documentation and workflow.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to power on or interact with the system. Observe: completely unresponsive system, stuck on boot screen or logo, freezing during operation or recording, delayed or partial startup. Expected: Issue is reproducible and clearly identified. Why it matters: Confirms whether this is a startup failure vs runtime freeze.

steps.title: Perform a Full Power Cycle (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Power down the system completely. Turn off using power button (hold if needed), disconnect AC power for 30–60 seconds, reconnect and restart. Expected: System boots normally after reset. Why it matters: Clears temporary system lockups or software hangs.

steps.title: Check AC Power Source and Power Stability
steps.instructions: Verify the system is connected to a stable power source. Test outlet with known-good device, avoid overloaded power strips, confirm power cable is secure. Expected: Stable power confirmed. Why it matters: Power fluctuations can cause boot failures or freezes.

steps.title: Disconnect External Devices (High-Impact Cause)
steps.instructions: Remove all non-essential peripherals. Disconnect USB drives, external storage, keyboards, or accessories, leaving only essential video and power connections. Expected: System boots normally without peripherals. Why it matters: Faulty or incompatible devices can hang the boot process.

steps.title: Check Network Connection (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Disconnect network cable temporarily. Expected: System boots without delay. Why it matters: Network initialization or DICOM communication can stall startup if misconfigured.

steps.title: Inspect Cooling and Ventilation
steps.instructions: Check for overheating conditions. Ensure vents are clear, confirm fans are running, remove any obstructions. Expected: Proper airflow and normal operating temperature. Why it matters: Overheating can cause system instability or shutdown.

steps.title: Observe for Error Messages or Indicators
steps.instructions: Watch for any on-screen errors during boot. Expected: Identification of specific fault (if present). Why it matters: Error messages can indicate software corruption or hardware failure.

steps.title: Attempt Restart After Minimal Configuration
steps.instructions: Reconnect only required components and reboot. Expected: Stable startup and operation. Why it matters: Confirms whether issue is related to external configuration.

CCR.Complaint: SDC Ultra system frozen on boot screen and not responding to input during OR setup.
CCR.Cause: System freeze caused by connected USB storage device interfering with startup process.
CCR.Resolution: Removed external USB device, performed power cycle, and verified normal system boot and operation.

helpfulDetails: Boot behavior observed (logo freeze, black screen, etc.)
